mission.title: Install and Configure Prometheus Operator / kube-prometheus-stack
mission.description: The Prometheus Operator simplifies the deployment and management of Prometheus monitoring stacks on Kubernetes. It leverages Kubernetes custom resources to automate configuration, making it easier to set up monitoring for applications. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes resources and potential complexity in advanced configurations.

steps.title: Add the Prometheus Operator Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Prometheus Operator Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add prometheus-community https://prometheus-community.github.io/helm-charts

```

> This is the official repository for the Prometheus Operator.

steps.title: Install the Prometheus Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Prometheus Operator using Helm, specifying the namespace and version.

```bash

helm install prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --create-namespace --version 0.77.0

```

> Change the version as needed.

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Prometheus Operator and its components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> You should see pods for Prometheus, Alertmanager, and Grafana.

steps.title: Create a Prometheus custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource definition for Prometheus to start monitoring your applications.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
EOF

```

> Replace 'my-app' with your application's label.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: monitoring.coreos.com/v1
kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Prometheus Operator
uninstall.description:
This command removes the Prometheus Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all monitoring data.

```bash

helm uninstall prometheus-operator --namespace monitoring

kubectl delete namespace monitoring

```

> This will remove the Helm release.
> This will delete the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Prometheus Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Prometheus data. Then, upgrade the Helm release and verify the installation.

```bash

helm upgrade prometheus-operator prometheus-community/kube-prometheus-stack --namespace monitoring --version 0.77.0

kubectl get pods -n monitoring

```

> Change the version as needed.
> Verify that all pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error creating service monitor'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This usually happens when the service monitor selector does not match any existing services.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the labels in your service match the selector in the service monitor.

troubleshooting.title: Prometheus is not scraping metrics
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the service monitor is not configured correctly or the target is down.

**Fix:**
Check the service monitor configuration and ensure the target application is running.

troubleshooting.title: Grafana dashboard not loading
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may happen if the Grafana service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Check the Grafana service type and ensure it is set to LoadBalancer or NodePort.

troubleshooting.title: Pods in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the pod is failing to start, often due to misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n monitoring' for more details.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new alerting rules and improved Grafana dashboards.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get pods -n monitoring' showing all pods in the Running state.
codeSnippets:

kind: Prometheus
metadata:
  name: example-prometheus
  namespace: monitoring
spec:
  replicas: 1
  serviceAccountName: prometheus-operator
  serviceMonitorSelector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
